title: CatBoost](https://www.kaggle.com/code/yekenot/ps-s6-e5-realmlp-pytabkit%22,%22https://www.kaggle.com/code/masayakawamata/s6e5-realmlp-with-fe%22]},{%22title%22:%22CatBoost) native categorical baseline
summary: Run a controlled CatBoost model that keeps Driver, Race, Compound, Year, and engineered context categories as native categorical features rather than only frequency or ordinal encodings.
rationale: The data contains high-cardinality categoricals with complete train/test overlap, and external notebooks include CatBoost as a core model. Native ordered target statistics can capture category effects while reducing manual encoding complexity when validation is configured correctly.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoostClassifier with categorical feature indices, moderate depth, early stopping, and grouped OOF folds. Compare raw categoricals only, raw plus frequency encodings, and raw plus fold-safe target encodings to determine whether manual encodings are additive.
expected_effect: Small to medium AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: CatBoost ordered statistics still depend on fold design and random seed. High-cardinality Driver interactions may overfit under random holdout.
